key_insight: A hybrid machine-learning pipeline using clinical text embeddings and TF-IDF can predict TNM staging from pathology reports, with the best results coming from LightGBM on TF-IDF features.
clinical_relevance: This work could help automate cancer staging extraction from pathology reports to support oncology workflows, but it is still a research baseline rather than a clinical tool.
method: Compared TF-IDF with ClinicalBERT, BioBERT, and PubMedBERT embeddings
Trained Logistic Regression, LightGBM, FFNN, and Wide Residual Networks
Evaluated T, N, and M staging as separate multi-label classification tasks
evidence_signal: In training, LightGBM with TF-IDF achieved the strongest AUROC and F1 across stages, while Codabench test results were strong but showed a drop in macro-F1 on the second test set.
watch_item: Generalizability remains a concern because performance declined on later test data, likely reflecting class imbalance and the difficulty of long clinical documents.
paper_title: CaresAI at SMM4H-HeaRD 2026: Predicting TNM Staging
